data.narrative:
Firefly Aerospace, a public-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Tom Markusic, is evaluated against a peer universe of 5 public and 8 private comparable companies, including Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, and Astroscale. The target's current valuation stands at $6.3B following an $868M IPO.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. Across three same-sector, same-stage peers—Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Astroscale ($900M)—the median valuation is $7.0B. This approach directly anchors Firefly's implied value at $7.0B. A public-comp P/S method cannot be applied; while the broader Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 36.9x median multiple across six public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), the target's revenue remains undisclosed, preventing revenue-based valuation.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $6.0B to $8.0B, centered at a median of $7.0B. Firefly's current $6.3B valuation sits below this median, approximately 10% below the peer-derived midpoint. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($900M to $37.0B), which reflects heterogeneity in commercialization stage and mission profile, and the absence of revenue data for precision valuation. Relative positioning within the peer range depends on unobserved operational and contractual differentials.
